task_completion.description: The agent documented the specific error causing the crash in the required text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output (e.g., cat) showing the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/reason_for_crash.txt. The text must mention the SYNC_BATCH_SIZE error (e.g., expecting an integer but receiving '100 items').

task_completion.description: The agent fixed the configuration error in docker-compose.yml.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing docker-compose.yml with the SYNC_BATCH_SIZE environment variable updated to a valid integer (e.g., '100' instead of '100 items').

task_completion.description: The agent modified the application or Dockerfile to send logs to stdout/stderr.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing the Python application code updated to log to sys.stdout/sys.stderr (e.g., using logging.StreamHandler), OR a Dockerfile modified to symlink the log file to /dev/stdout and /dev/stderr.

task_completion.description: The agent rebuilt and restarted the container so it runs stably.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing Docker build/start commands (e.g., docker-compose up -d --build) and a subsequent 'docker ps' command showing the 'acme-sync-worker' container with a status of 'Up' (not restarting or exited).

task_completion.description: The agent verified that logs are visible via the standard docker logs command.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the execution of 'docker logs acme-sync-worker' (or similar) returning visible log entries from the application, confirming the silent crash issue is resolved.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely modified the configuration and application files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of file editing using a text editor (nano, vim, VS Code) or valid shell redirection, rather than just printing the expected final state to the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent actually interacted with the Docker daemon to rebuild and check logs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Real Docker CLI output showing build steps, container status, and log output. The output should look like standard Docker formatting, not manually typed or mocked text.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the docker logs output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The logs shown in 'docker logs' should match the format of a standard Python application logging output, and the container must genuinely be running in the 'docker ps' output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project directory: /home/ga/projects/acme-sync. Container name: acme-sync-worker. Internal log path: /var/log/acme/sync.log. Desktop report path: /home/ga/Desktop/reason_for_crash.txt. The specific error causing the crash is that SYNC_BATCH_SIZE expects an integer but receives '100 items'. The fix requires changing this value in docker-compose.yml to an integer (e.g., 100) and modifying the Python app or Dockerfile to route logs to stdout/stderr.
